chunks.page_summary: This page provides a technical guide on integrating Rheonics' Smart Module Electronics (SME) with CODESYS controllers using the Ethernet/IP communication protocol, outlining general concepts, prerequisites, and configuration steps.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Ethernet/IP Communication through CODESYS

Modified on: Thu, 17 Aug, 2023 at 3:31 PM

*   Which Rheonics products are related to this article?
    This article is based on the use of the SME (Smart Module Electronics) from SRV (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/), SRD (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/), DVP (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-gas-lng-density-meter-dvp/ and DVM (https://rheonics.com/products/hpht-pvt-density-meter-viscometer-dvm/). Hence it can be used for any of these Rheonics sensors.
*   What is the purpose of this article?
    To give guidance on the integration of SME with a controller based on Codesys using an Ethernet/IP communication protocol.

TABLE OF CONTENTS

*   1. General Concepts
    *   1.1 Ethernet/IP Overview
    *   1.2. CODESYS Overview
    *   1.3. Prerequisite:
    *   1.4. SME Ethernet/IP connection
*   2. Configuration steps for Ethernet/IP on CODESYS
    *   2.1. Creating the project
    *   2.2. Installing the EDS file in Codesys.
    *   2.3. Select the controller device
    *   2.4. Configure Ethernet Interface and Ethernet/IP Controller on CODESYS
*   Troubleshooting
*   Related Articles
*   References

1. General Concepts

1.1 Ethernet/IP Overview

Ethernet/IP is one of the most commonly used industrial communication protocols, it enables data exchange between controllers and devices based on international standards. To link all the components in its network, Ethernet/IP requires standard Ethernet as the communication medium. It can operate on higher transmission speeds than other protocols and requires names and IP addresses for all devices.

1.2. CODESYS Overview

CODESYS (Controller Development System) is a manufacturer-independent software for industrial automation technology. It follows the IEC-61131-3 standard to program intelligent devices and controllers. It is compatible with multiple device types and controllers. You can see which companies offer CODESYS-compatible devices here (https://www.codesys.com/the-system/codesys-inside.html).

1.3. Prerequisite:

CODESYS software installed
key_facts: The article relates to Rheonics' Smart Module Electronics (SME) and sensors including SRV, SRD, DVP, and DVM.
The purpose of this article is to guide the integration of SME with CODESYS controllers using the Ethernet/IP communication protocol.
Ethernet/IP is a widely used industrial communication protocol that facilitates data exchange between controllers and devices.
Ethernet/IP utilizes standard Ethernet as its communication medium and requires IP addresses and names for all connected devices.
CODESYS (Controller Development System) is manufacturer-independent software for industrial automation, compliant with the IEC-61131-3 standard.
CODESYS is compatible with various device types and controllers for programming intelligent devices.
A prerequisite for this guide is that CODESYS software must be installed.

chunks.page_summary: This page outlines the requirements and initial configuration steps for setting up a Rheonics sensor with Ethernet/IP communication using CODESYS, including a communication diagram.
chunks.extracted_text:
- Rheonics Sensor Electronics (SME) with Ethernet and Ethernet/IP enabled
- Rheonics EDS files. (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000393236-ethernetip-eds-file-for-rheonics-inline-viscometer-and-density-meter)
- The IP address from the Rheonics SME - this can be read out from the SME using RCP software (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000397449-verifying-the-network-setup-on-the-pc-where-the-rcp-software-is-installed)
- CODESYS-compatible controller.

1.4. SME Ethernet/IP connection
The Rheonics sensor is compatible with Ethernet/IP networks. For that, the Ethernet RJ45 port is used and it can be connected directly to the client's PC or through a router, switch, or hub.

Figure 1: Ethernet/IP Communication Diagram.

2. Configuration steps for Ethernet/IP on CODESYS

2.1. Creating the project

1. Click on the create project button
key_facts: Rheonics Sensor Electronics (SME) must have Ethernet and Ethernet/IP enabled.
Rheonics EDS files are available for Rheonics inline viscometer and density meter.
The IP address of the Rheonics SME can be read using RCP software.
A CODESYS-compatible controller is required for the setup.
The Rheonics sensor connects to Ethernet/IP networks via an Ethernet RJ45 port.
The sensor can be connected directly to a client's PC or through a router, switch, or hub.
The first step in configuring Ethernet/IP on CODESYS is to create a new project.

The 'Standard Project' option must be selected from the templates.
An example project name is 'Rheonics Ethernet/IP' saved at 'D:\Users\Administrator\Documents\ethernetio'.
The 'Control for Raspberry Pi SL device' needs to be selected in a subsequent step, which will then appear in the device tree.

Figure 4. Selecting the device controller

2.2. Installing the EDS file in Codesys.

The eds file will allow the controller to understand the way in which the data coming from the SME is structured (Figure 14) and define each parameter from the SME. Not doing this step will require the programmer to manually set the configuration from the sensor.

Download the EDS files from Rheonics Support Page (https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000401423-profinet-gsd-file-for-rheonics-inline-viscometer-and-density-meter) and install them in the CODESYS Device Repository with the next steps:

1. Go to "Device Repository" in the Tools Menu.
2. Select the "Edit Locations" option.
3, 4. Click on "Add" and indicate the path to the EDS files on the PC, write a name for the file, and click OK.

key_facts: EDS files enable the controller to understand the data structure and parameters from the SME (Sensor/Meter Equipment).
Without EDS files, manual configuration of the sensor by the programmer is required.
EDS files for Rheonics inline viscometers and density meters can be downloaded from the Rheonics Support Page.
The installation process involves adding EDS files to the CODESYS Device Repository.
A standard project in CODESYS includes a programmable device, a PLC_PRG program (in Ladder Logic Diagram), a cyclic task, and a reference to the standard library.
The default device controller for the standard project is "CODESYS Control for Raspberry Pi SL (CODESYS GmbH)".
diagram_labels: CODESYS Control for Raspberry Pi SL (CODESYS GmbH)
warnings_notes: Not doing this step will require the programmer to manually set the configuration from the sensor.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions on how to add Rheonics EDS files to the Codesys Device Repository and then configure a controller device for an Ethernet/IP network.
chunks.extracted_text:
5. Select the EDS file, that matches the sensor that is being used for your application.

[CODESYS Software Interface]
Rheonics sensors

Figure 6. Adding Rheonics eds files to the Codesys "Device Repository"

2.3. Select the controller device
With a Project created on Codesys, follow the next steps to set up the controller and establish an Ethernet/IP Network.
1. Double-click on the Device (previously selected when creating the Project).
2. Click on "Communication Settings".
3. Select the Gateway by default.
4. Click on "Scan Network" after selecting the gateway, this will open a pop-out window.
5, 6. With the scan process, the controller connected to the network should be shown. Select it and click OK.
key_facts: Users must select the correct EDS file corresponding to the sensor used in their application.
Rheonics EDS files are added to the Codesys "Device Repository" for managing device descriptions.
Setting up the controller and establishing an Ethernet/IP Network involves specific steps within Codesys.
The process includes configuring communication settings, selecting a gateway, and scanning the network to identify connected controllers.
Once the network scan is complete, the connected controller must be selected and confirmed by clicking OK.

key_facts: The CODESYS software environment is used to manage a Rheonics Profinet project.
A Raspberry Pi controller, identified as 'CODESYS Control for Raspberry Pi SL', is configured in the system.
The initial setup involves scanning the network to select the Raspberry Pi device and viewing its communication parameters.
The controller is connected via a gateway with IP-Address 'localhost' and Port '1217'.
The Raspberry Pi controller uses UDP as its block driver and supports TLS for encrypted communication.
Key identification details for the Raspberry Pi controller include Device Address '0033', Serial number '10000000eed4074b', Target ID '0000 0010', Target Type '4102', and Target Version '4.4.0.0'.
The vendor for the target controller is 3S - Smart Software Solutions GmbH.
An option exists to enable automatic updates of received values without requiring explicit parameter calls in the program.
diagram_labels: Target Vendor: 3S - Smart Software Solutions GmbH

2.4. Configure Ethernet Interface and Ethernet/IP Controller on CODESYS
1. Right-click on Device and select add new Device.
2. In the next window look for Fieldbuses, search for Ethernet/IP -> Ethernet Adapter -> Ethernet.
3. Finally, click on “Add device”.
key_facts: The page details how to configure an Ethernet Interface and Ethernet/IP Controller using the CODESYS software.
The configuration process involves navigating to the 'PLC Settings' within the 'Device' context.
Users need to right-click on 'Device' and select 'add new Device' to begin the setup.
The path for adding the Ethernet device is Fieldbuses -> Ethernet/IP -> Ethernet Adapter -> Ethernet.
Changing the 'Always update variables' option in PLC settings can prevent future login without a download, prompting a warning for user confirmation.
Various PLC settings related to I/O handling, variable updates, and bus cycle options are configurable.
Additional settings include enabling diagnosis for devices and symbolic access for I/Os.

key_facts: Rheonics DVP and SRV Viscosity Meters can be discovered on an Ethernet/IP network within CODESYS.
Multiple SRV Viscosity Meter devices (up to 6 shown) can be scanned and added to a project.
The 'Copy All Devices to Project' function integrates scanned Rheonics sensors into the CODESYS device tree.
CODESYS provides notifications about the operational status of connected electronics when online with the controller.

Troubleshooting
Check for common problems with Ethernet/IP and probable solutions at Ethernet/IP troubleshooting (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000402741-ethernet-ip-troubleshooting)

key_facts: The page illustrates the CODESYS software interface in online mode, showing variable monitoring for Rheonics devices.
Rheonics viscometers and density sensors are integrated and monitored using CODESYS via Ethernet/IP.
The document provides troubleshooting resources for common Ethernet/IP problems encountered with Rheonics products.
Detailed process variables, including viscosity, density, and temperature, are accessible and displayed in the CODESYS interface.
The 'Temperature Median Value Parameter 2' is currently reading 26.484, reflecting real-time process data.
Rheonics offers support articles regarding data point access and connecting Ethernet/IP output for their devices.
diagram_labels: Device (connected) [CODESYS Control for Raspberry Pi SL]
